title: What you must know before Mythos arrives | Nebulock, Damien Lewke
summary: Damien Lewke argues that AI has drastically reduced the barrier to launching cyberattacks, enabling small teams with just a GPU to execute sophisticated campaigns that previously required elite teams and years of expertise. He outlines the cyber kill chain stages, identifies three behavioral signals that reveal active attackers inside networks, and advocates for an "assume breach" defensive posture as the only realistic security model.
writeup: Damien Lewke argues that AI has lowered the barrier to launching cyberattacks, letting small teams with a single GPU run sophisticated campaigns that once required elite operators and years of expertise. He walks through the stages of the cyber kill chain and names three behavioral signals that expose active attackers already inside a network. The central takeaway is a case for an "assume breach" posture, treating detection and threat hunting through behavioral auditing as the realistic security model rather than perimeter defense alone.
